mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows, designed for self-hosting on Kubernetes. It provides a robust set of features for building AI applications, but requires careful resource management and configuration to ensure optimal performance.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Dify Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dify using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.langgenius.com

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Dify Helm chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
Before installing Dify, you need to install its Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) to ensure Kubernetes recognizes the Dify resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for Dify.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Dify on your Kubernetes cluster using the specified namespace and version.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> Replace '0.12.0' with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
After installing Dify, you need to create an example Custom Resource (CR) to start using the platform.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-difyapp
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample Dify application.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-difyapp
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dify installation
steps.description:
Ensure that the Dify application is running correctly by checking the status of the pods.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dify

```

> This command lists all pods in the 'dify' namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step removes Dify from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This command uninstalls the Dify release.
> This command deletes the 'dify' namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This process involves draining the pods, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.0

kubectl get pods -n dify

```

> Replace <pod-name> with the name of the Dify pod to drain.
> Replace '0.12.0' with the desired version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'DifyApp not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified DifyApp resource does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the DifyApp resource was created successfully and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image is not available or the image tag is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in the Dify deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dify application is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust the configuration as needed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not reachable or is overloaded.

**Fix:**
Check the network connectivity and the status of the Kubernetes cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new workflow features and improved API performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs that are no longer supported.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.11, review the migration guide for changes in resource definitions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dify application running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n dify'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-difyapp
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with the required resources and tools installed.
